originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and tesseract scripts, then initializes many inline 4D geometry, projection, and slicing sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the major geometry sections mount without runtime errors.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a slider-driven tesseract projection scene, then builds from a point to a line, square, cube, and tesseract through early slider and drag-driven construction scenes.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening projection slider plus the early construction sections that progressively build higher-dimensional cubes from lower-dimensional ones.
notes: The opening and early construction scenes still rely on the original Drawer and Slider runtime rather than a local rewrite.
The route keeps the published sequence from lower-dimensional construction to rotatable 3D cube examples.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one opening slider scene and one early construction scene as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening tesseract scene redraws from slider input and an early construction scene updates from its published slider.

originalBehavior: The published article continues into ambiguous-view examples, rotation-plane scenes, xw-plane tesseract rotation, and the later 3D slicing payoff with multiple sliders and drag-driven viewing-angle changes.
localStatus: The local route keeps the ambiguous-view scene, the plane-of-rotation section, the xw rotation scene, and the later 3D slice demonstrations driven by the original article runtime.
notes: The later scenes still rely on the original slider, segmented-control, and drag behaviors rather than custom local controls.
The route preserves the published order of projection, shadow, plane-of-rotation, in-and-out, and final slicing sections.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one ambiguous-view drag scene, one plane-of-rotation slider scene, and one later intersection or slicing scene as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a drag-driven perspective scene updates, a rotation-plane control redraws, and a later 3D slice scene remains interactive.

originalBehavior: The published page relies on article-shell helper sprites and shared control icons while also exposing author promo links, RSS, and supporter surfaces around the article shell.
localStatus: The local copy vendors the helper sprites locally, keeps the interactive geometry scenes intact, and leaves the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The route does not require upstream media requests at runtime beyond the local helper assets and published scripts.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks author-promo, analytics, and supporter surfaces from shipping in the route body.
Shared smoke coverage and route checks verify the route loads locally without upstream asset requests while keeping the interactive controls usable.
